It is evident that
both these forms of regularization on unlabeled data have comple-
mentary benefits and improve the overall performance significantly

concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
We conduct extensive experiments on Polyvore and Polyvore-
Disjoint [43] datasets and show that our proposed approach can
achieve on-par performance with fully supervised methods even
with 5% of labeled outfits.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Finally, we create a large scale Fashion Outfits
dataset consisting of around 700K outfits with more than 3M im-
ages and demonstrate consistent improvement in performance over
fully-supervised baseline.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
As it is computationally infeasible
to perform nearest neighbor search on entire unlabeled dataset
D𝑈, we randomly sample sufficiently large mini-batch of unlabeled
images, b𝑢, to generate pseudo-outfits and finally impose margin
loss (L𝑝𝑠𝑒𝑢𝑑𝑜) on the pseudo-triplets ( ˜𝐴, ˜𝑃, ˜𝑁) as shown in Fig.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
The representa-
tions show that our embedding space can effectively capture appearance information (such as color).
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Another interesting point to note is that, at full supervision
(i.e.𝛼= 100%), our approach achieves 0.89 AUC outperforming
many supervised approaches on the compatibility task compared
in Table 2 due to the added consistency regularization supervision.
concepts.implication: Can inform intent modeling for recommendation systems

concepts.finding:
First, to learn important attributes such as color, we
introduced a self-supervision scheme that enforces consistency in
the representation of input and its random transformations.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Additionally, we
introduce consistency regularization to ensure that representation
of the original images and their transformations are consistent
to implicitly incorporate colour and other important attributes
through self-supervision.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
We conduct extensive experiments on
Polyvore, Polyvore-D and our newly created large-scale Fashion
Outfits datasets, and show that our approach with only a fraction
of labeled examples performs on-par with completely supervised
methods.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
In a fully supervised setting, our ap-
proach can achieve state-of-the-art performance on compatibility
prediction task.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
• We introduce consistency regularization and pseudo-labeling
based data augmentation techniques to learn different at-
tributes and generate pseudo-outfits, completely from the
unlabeled fashion images.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Fashion Compatibility introduced by Han et al.[15] was formu-
lated as a sequential problem and trained a bi-directional recurrent
network model that predicts the next compatible item conditioned
on previously observed items in the outfit sequence.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Further, Lin et al.[24]
introduce a fashion retrieval problem for images and learn a type-
dependent attention mechanism.
concepts.implication: Architecture pattern applicable to sequence modeling for user behavior

concepts.finding:
Generative adversarial network (GAN)
[11] based approaches have also been proposed for semi-supervised
learning, however these are challenging to train [38].
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
concepts.open_question:
How to address the limitation described: Generative adversarial network (GAN)
[11] based approaches have also been proposed for semi-supervised
learning, however these are challenging to train [38].

concepts.finding:
This is achieved through metric learn-
ing by bringing the embedding of items (from different categories)
that go well together in a outfit closer compared to non-compatible
items.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Our proposed approach is a data augmentation technique where
pseudo-positive and negative triplet pairs (Fig 2(b)) are generated
on-the-fly during each training iteration based on visual similarity
of individual examples in the triplet using the images in the unla-
beled set.
concepts.implication: Relevant for similarity-based retrieval and lookalike targeting

concepts.finding:
We next formulate the problem of semi-supervised fashion com-
patibility and then describe our proposed data augmentation tech-
niques in greater detail.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation
